page: Amazon Web Services c8a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs utilizing the AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2821.0 and a multi-core score of 28165.0, both placing in the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 6812.8 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 128.56 GB/sec. Cryptographic and vector operations are accelerated via AVX-512 and AES instruction sets, resulting in strong performance in Passmark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8a.8xlarge offers high compute density and memory speed, making it highly cost-effective for CPU-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is well-suited for high-throughput static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference tasks, such as text generation using 7B parameter models.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2821.0 and a multi-core score of 28165.0. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput capabilities in database operations and static web serving. This instance is highly cost-effective for compute-intensive workloads, making it an excellent fit for software compilation, high-performance web serving, database operations, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core and multi-core performance.

tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and LLM inference
